examples.title: Create Directory Entry
examples.description: Algorithm to create a new entry in directory structure
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CreateDirectoryEntry(dirName, fileInfo)
BEGIN
  IF directoryFull THEN
    RETURN ERROR_NO_SPACE
  ENDIF
  
  // Check for name conflicts
  FOR each entry IN currentDirectory DO
    IF entry.name == dirName THEN
      RETURN ERROR_NAME_EXISTS
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  
  // Allocate new directory entry
  newEntry.name = dirName
  newEntry.attributes = fileInfo
  newEntry.inode = allocateInode()
  newEntry.created = currentTime()
  
  // Add to directory structure
  appendToDirectory(newEntry)
  updateDirectorySize()
  
  RETURN SUCCESS
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the process of creating a new directory entry, including checking for name conflicts and allocating necessary data structures.

examples.title: Directory Search Implementation
examples.explanation: Demonstrates two directory search methods: linear O(n) search and hash-based O(1) average search with collision handling.

examples.title: Path Resolution Algorithm
examples.description: Resolve absolute and relative paths in hierarchical directory
examples.explanation: C implementation showing how to resolve both absolute and relative paths by traversing directory tree, handling "." (current) and ".." (parent) navigation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(d) where d is path depth

examples.title: Directory Permissions Management
examples.description: Implement access control for directory operations
examples.explanation: Java implementation of directory access control showing owner/group/others permission model similar to UNIX file permissions.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of access rules
